channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: WWE 2K26 Patch 1.04 Fixes Ringside Pass XP Exploit
item.content:encoded:
WWE 2K26‘s latest patch is live. Here are the full details of the problems that the latest patch addresses.

Visual Concepts has released the patch notes for WWE 2K26 Patch 1.04. Of note, the main talking point online after this patch was announced was the note about fixing an XP glitch to level up in the Ringside Pass faster.

Many players aren’t happy with the extra cost of the new content release system, and the latest patch claims to have fixed the exploit being used to level up quicker and unlock more items.

WWE 2K26 Patch 1.04 Full Notes

General
– Stability and Performance improvements
– Addressed reported concerns over earnable RXP
– Addressed reported concerns over Game Pass subscriptions being required to access – The Island and MyFaction
– Addressed reported concerns over 2K account linking occasionally not working
– Addressed reported concerns with Dominik reward mask
– Addressed reported concerns of various attacks not working as intended

Island
– Addressed reported concerns over performance issues when playing The Island

Ringside Pass Adjustments

These are the full notes that Visual Concepts shared about the ringside pass adjustments with the new patch. These adjustments go live today on PlayStation, PC, and Xbox, but will come to Nintendo Switch on March 30.

So far we’ve seen the player experience not being exactly what’s intended, so we’re implementing some changes with Patch 1.04, including:

Custom Matches will now award RXP
– RXP earn rates for matches increased by 20%
– Match Complete: 25 (current) > 30
– Match Win Bonus: 85 (current) > 105
– Minimum match time requirement added for Match Win RXP rewards
– If a match is completed faster than 60 seconds, players will receive completion RXP, but not additional win bonuses. We know you’re enjoying making Eric Bischoff tap out, but let’s give poor “Eazy E” a breather.

Learn more about the Ringside Pass system at this link. Insider Gaming will continue to provide the latest updates on the latest WWE 2K title as they become available.

If you’re already playing it, are you enjoying WWE 2K26 so far? Have you encountered any of the bugs mentioned above? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/wwe-2k26-patch-1-04-fixes-ringside-pass-xp-exploit/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: League of Legends Patch 26.6 Full Preview
item.content:encoded:
Patch 26.6 for League of Legends releases next week, and Riot Games has given a full preview of what gameplay updates fans can expect.

Riot claims that the game is currently in a healthy state in terms of balance and player satisfaction, though things will likely shift with the upcoming release of the Shyvana rework. Cassiopeia, Karthus, and Xerath had been set for changes this patch, but have now been removed in the latest preview.

Here are all the upcoming champion buffs and nerfs coming in the next LoL patch, which releases on March 18, as well as explanations from lead gameplay designer Matt ‘Phroxzon’ Leung-Harrison.

Upcoming LoL Patch 26.6 Champion Buffs

Azir

Phroxon said: “We’re buffing Azir to distribute some of his power back into regular play capabilities through scaling power that Azir players hopefully will appreciate.”

  • [Q] Conquering Sands AP ratio increased 35% -> 55%
  • [W] Arise! damage increased 0-55 (based on levels 9-20, linear) (+50/65/80/95/110) (+32.5/40/47.5/55/62.5% AP) -> 0-72 (based on levels 9-20, linear) (+50/65/80/95/110) (+35/42.5/50/57.5/65% AP)

Lissandra

Phroxon revealed that Riot are reverting the nerfs to Lissandra’s Q that were made back in Season 19.

  • Armor per level reduced 4.9 -> 4.2
  • [Q] Ice Shard cooldown reduced 8/7/6/5/4 -> 7/6/5/4/3 seconds

Olaf

These changes are primarily aimed at boosting Olaf in the jungle but he should benefit in the top lane too.

  • Base Attack Speed increased 0.694 -> 0.72
  • [Q] Undertow slow adjusted 25/30/35/40/45% for 1.5-2.5 seconds -> 30/35/40/45/50% for 1-3 seconds (based on distance axe travelled 400-1000)
  • [E] Reckless Swing HP cost increased 21/34.5/48/61.5/75 (+15% AD) -> 28/46/64/82/100 (+20% AD)

Skarner

Phroxon claims Skarner hasn’t been in the spotlight for a while and after nerfs stopped him from dominating top, Riot believes there is now room for buffs.

  • [Q1/Q2] Shattered Earth/Upheaval buffs:
    • Target’s HP damage on third hit/on-recast increased 9% -> 11%
    • Mana cost reduced 45 -> 30

Tryndamere

This is a rather small buff that Phroxon didn’t offer any additional context for.

  • Attack Speed ratio increased 0.694 -> 0.725

Upcoming LoL Patch 26.6 Champion Nerfs

Ahri

  • Q] Orb of Deception base damage per hit reduced 40/65/90/115/140 -> 35/60/85/110/135

Pyke

  • Base Armor reduced 43-> 37

Shen

Shen became surprisingly strong in the jungle following a new build popularised by streamer xPetu. Phroxon thanked the content creator for improving Shen’s popularity, but claims the champion has become too strong, especially at lower ranks.

  • [Q] Twilight Assault maximum monster damage reduced 120/140/160/180/200 -> 100/125/150/175/200

Zaahen

Phroxon says he’s happy to see that Zaahen has picked up a strong audience but concedes the champion is too strong, especially now that regular players are beginning to master him.

Riot believes that Zaahen is more of an issue in the jungle than top, so these nerfs are focused there.

  • [W] Dreaded Return second hit damage reduced 40/60/80/100/120 (+50% bAD) -> 30/50/70/90/110 (+30% bAD) (total damage reduced 80/120/160/200/240 (+100% bAD) -> 70/110/150/190/230 (+80% bAD))
  • [E] Aureate Rush bonus monster damage reduced 75 -> 50

Upcoming LoL Patch 26.6 Item changes

Chempunk Chainsword

  • Cost reduced 3100 -> 3000 gold

Sunfire Aegis

  • Build path changed Bami’s Cinder + Chain Vest + 1000 gold -> Bami’s Cinder + Chain Vest + Ruby Crystal + 600 gold

Support Item Penalty

  • Maximum minion kills before 5 minutes increased 7 -> 24
  • Maximum minion kills per minute after 5 minutes increased 4 -> 6

Are you happy with the current meta in League of Legends? Share your thoughts with us in the Insider Gaming Discord.

And get ready for even bigger changes to the MOBA, as League of Legends is getting voice chat.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/league-of-legends-patch-26-6-full-preview/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Escape from Tarkov ‘Realistic Aiming’ Changes Don’t Land Well With Fans
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has previewed some ‘realistic non-linear aiming’ changes coming to Escape from Tarkov, the pioneering extraction shooter with a decade in the business. In a post on social media, Nikita Buyanov, the head of the studio at Battlestate, showcased more than a minute of old vs. new footage, comparing the ‘reworked’ aiming mechanics to what exists now.

However, the changes have not landed well with the bulk of the community, particularly the infamously vocal streamers who have cut their teeth broadcasting Escape from Tarkov content for years.

Escape from Tarkov’s Aiming Changes Miss the Mark

In the post on social media, Nikita Buyanov shared what’s coming next for Escape from Tarkov’s aiming palette:

He followed up with a comment:

always wanted to have this approach to aiming in EFT. cause right now it’s unrealistic and linear. We will refine it more and test it first in ETS.

By ETS, he means ‘Early Test Server’, the platform that users can access to trial changes coming to Escape from Tarkov’s main platform at some point (or perhaps not).

Despite pledging more realism, the changes have gone down the other way with fans, especially those familiar with firearms. For instance, Airwingmarine wrote:

But legit, this looks nothing like what we do IRL. And high end shooters with lots of reps (PMCs) were actually pretty close to what we had in Tarkov. Maybe not for LPVOs and scopes, but for red dots and holos.

That weird set animation look super unnatural to me.

In another comment, LVNDMARK, one of the most prolific and popular Escape from Tarkov streamers, wrote:

Realism =\= fun in a video game nor do I think this is even realistic. You guys should be taking away a lot of the mechanics that were added to make this game feel more clunky rather than adding more. Just my 2 cents.

In the comments under Nikita’s post, some even said they would be uninstalling the game, at the insistence that Battlestate Games is out to make things as ‘unfun’ as possible.

But then, hours later, Nikita addressed the backlash:

guys, understood and got all of the feedback related WIP changes to aiming.

Take it easy, there will be another iteration and there will be ETS test first. If you still will not like it – than I will double down and add it ingame asap!

I’m kidding. Just let it be seen and tested first with the new changes to balance it out. Hopefully it will add more immersion and you will like it. Don’t worry, it’s being done by single teammember and me.

Battlestate Games is known for being relatively experimental, and not everything it reveals makes it into the final product.

Do you think this change would be a win or a loss for Escape from Tarkov players? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Marathon is getting an esports tournament

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Strands #738 Hints and Answers for Today, March 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #738 for Wednesday, March 11, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 11, 2026

The Strands’ answers to Game #738 all focus on different defense mechanisms. Not all relate to physical battles, however.

General Strands Hints For March 11

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Knights wear this shiny protective gear.
  • Hint 2: Military servicemembers wear this
  • Hint 3: This is a strong upside to those who are quick.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #738 for March 11, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 11, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • CAME
  • FLAG
  • ARTS
  • ROMA
  • LITE

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #738 On March 11, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • DISTRACTION
  • ARMOR
  • AGILITY
  • MIMICRY
  • CAMOUFLAGE
  • SPANAGRAM: DEFENSES

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #738?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 12 will be Strands Game #739.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
